examples.title: Basic Class Definition
examples.description: Demonstrates how to define a simple class with data members and member functions
examples.explanation: This example shows a Rectangle class with private data members (length, breadth), a parameterized constructor, member functions to set dimensions and calculate area/perimeter, and a destructor. The main function demonstrates creating objects and calling member functions.

examples.title: Accessing Members Using this Pointer
examples.description: Demonstrates the use of this pointer to resolve naming conflicts
examples.explanation: The this pointer is used here to distinguish between the parameter 'width' and the member variable 'width'. It also demonstrates returning the object's address using this pointer.

examples.title: Static Data Members
examples.description: Demonstrates static data members shared across all objects
examples.explanation: Static data member objCount is shared by all objects. It persists throughout program execution and is initialized outside the class. The static function getObjCount() can be called using class name without creating an object.

examples.title: Friend Function
examples.description: Demonstrates friend function accessing private members
examples.explanation: Friend functions are non-member functions that have access to private and protected members of a class. They are declared using 'friend' keyword inside the class. Similarly, a friend class can access all private members of the class that grants friendship.
